title: Dali CitationIntegrityResult (Tier 1)
description: Schema for a single CitationIntegrityResult record produced by dali/runners/run_integrity.py against the canonical case corpus. One record per scored corpus case. Distinct from the Tier 2 result-record schema in data/results/v0.2/schema.json (which is one record per prompt × model pair).

case_id.description: Canonical case identifier (matches id in the corpus record).

citation_exists.description: Whether the cited authority actually exists.

authority_reachable.description: Whether the cited authority resolves to a reachable source.

semantic_alignment.description: Alignment between the citation and the proposition it supports.

quote_fidelity.description: Fidelity of any quoted material to the cited source.

temporal_validity.description: Whether the cited authority was valid at the time of the citing workflow.

jurisdiction_match.description: Whether the cited authority is from a jurisdiction relevant to the proposition.

provenance_complete.description: Whether the workflow that produced the citation has complete provenance.

failure_classes_detected.description: List of citation integrity failure classes triggered by this case.

defensibility_risk.description: Severity of the evidentiary defensibility risk created by the failure.

workflow_reconstructable.description: Whether the citation pathway can be reconstructed after the fact. Tri-state: true / false / 'unknown'.

verification_recoverable.description: Whether the workflow could realistically recover from the failure (e.g. manual, automated, infeasible).

mutation_lineage.description: Mutation chain applied during derivative case construction (empty for unmodified canonical cases). Items are parent case_id strings or structured mutation records.

policy_version.description: Composite policy version string covering taxonomy, rubric, scoring, normalization, and schema sub-versions.

evidence_hash.description: SHA-256 over (case_id, policy_version, run_timestamp). Per-run tamper-evident seal; differs across runs by design (timestamp is part of the seal). For the replay invariant see replay_hash.

corpus_record_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of the input corpus record alone. Independent of policy version; changes only when the record content itself changes. Detects silent mutation of the input corpus.

replay_hash.description: SHA-256 over (canonical corpus record, policy_version, source_document_hash). Replay invariant: identical inputs under identical policy always yield the same hash. Two runs of the runner against the same corpus and policy MUST produce byte-identical replay_hash values per case. Enforced by --verify-replay.

run_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the evaluation run.
